stories.title: OpenAI bets on families as ChatGPT goes deeper into households
stories.simple_summary: OpenAI is hiring someone to make ChatGPT work better for families and older adults. They want to design features that are easier for these groups to use.
stories.why_it_matters: If you're older or help care for parents or grandparents, ChatGPT may soon be simpler to use, similar to how some phones have bigger text options for older eyes.

stories.title: Meta removes controversial AI feature on Instagram after backlash
stories.simple_summary: Instagram removed a new AI tool after people complained about it. The tool let users create fake images of real people without their permission.
stories.why_it_matters: If you post on Instagram, your photos won't be used to create fake images of you anymore — Instagram listened to complaints and fixed the problem.

stories.title: Meta turns off the Instagram feature that let users make AI deepfakes of public accounts
stories.simple_summary: Meta (Instagram's owner) turned off the same AI deepfake feature mentioned above because people were upset that it could copy anyone's public photos without asking.
stories.why_it_matters: Your Instagram photos are now safer because the company stopped letting the AI use them to make fake versions without your permission.

stories.title: Apple sues OpenAI for allegedly stealing hardware secrets
stories.simple_summary: Apple is suing OpenAI, claiming that some OpenAI employees who used to work at Apple stole Apple's secrets to help OpenAI build hardware products.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors and big tech companies — it's a legal fight between giants that doesn't affect what you can do with AI.

stories.title: Apple sues OpenAI over alleged trade secret theft
stories.simple_summary: Apple alleges that OpenAI's leadership directed employees to steal Apple's trade secrets for hardware development.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors — it's a corporate lawsuit between two tech companies with nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: Open source AI matters more than ever, according to Hugging Face’s Clem Delangue
stories.simple_summary: Open source AI (AI that anyone can use and modify, like sharing a recipe) is growing fast. A company called Hugging Face has become like a library where people share AI tools, and many big companies use them.
stories.why_it_matters: As open source AI spreads, more small businesses may be able to use powerful AI tools without paying expensive companies, similar to how free software has helped small businesses before.

stories.title: SK Hynix raises $26.5B in the biggest foreign IPO in US history, is urged to build new US fabs
stories.simple_summary: A chip company called SK Hynix raised a huge amount of money on the stock market. People are now asking them to build computer chip factories in the United States.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors and people in tech manufacturing — it's about big business deals and factory building, not something that affects your daily use of AI.

stories.title: Hugging Face’s CEO on why companies are done renting their AI
stories.simple_summary: Companies are increasingly choosing to own their own AI tools instead of renting them from big companies. Open source AI (free AI anyone can use) is making this possible.
stories.why_it_matters: Small businesses might save money in the future by using free or owned AI tools instead of paying subscription fees to big tech companies.

stories.title: Instagram’s Adam Mosseri: If you don’t like AI, ‘then you shouldn’t have it in your feed’
stories.simple_summary: Instagram's leader says he won't filter out AI-made content from your feed, but he says you should be able to turn it off if you want to see less of it.
stories.why_it_matters: You may soon get better control over whether you see AI-generated posts on Instagram, so you won't have to look at fake content if you don't want to.

stories.title: Would you host part of an AI data center in your home?
stories.simple_summary: A solar company is offering to pay homeowners to put small AI computer equipment in their homes. This equipment would help run AI services without needing big data centers.
stories.why_it_matters: If your home has good space and power, you could earn some money by hosting this equipment — it's like renting out part of your garage, but the company handles everything.

stories.title: OpenAI says GPT 5.6 is the ‘preferred model’ for Microsoft Copilot 365 amid breakup chatter
stories.simple_summary: OpenAI's newest AI model will keep powering Microsoft's Copilot tools (the AI features in Microsoft Office and other products).
stories.why_it_matters: If you use Microsoft Word or Excel, your AI features will continue working with the latest technology — no changes needed on your end.

stories.title: Microsoft’s carbon emissions went up 25 percent last year
stories.simple_summary: Microsoft's greenhouse gas emissions went up by 25 percent last year, mostly because they're building more AI data centers that use lots of electricity.
stories.why_it_matters: All this AI growth uses a lot of electricity, which is a concern for the environment — it's worth knowing that the technology has a real cost to our planet.
